title: Peaceful protest for SA engineers illegally detained in Equatorial Guinea
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The families of Frik...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - The families of Frik Potgieter and Peter Huxham will be holding a peaceful protest at Africa Energy Week in Cape Town, tomorrow, Tuesday 5 November.

The protest aims to appeal to representatives of Equatorial Guinea, who will be in attendance at the conference, to consider clemency for the two men who have been unlawfully detained in Equatorial Guinea since 9 February 2023 (over 634 days on Tuesday).

The families will also be appealing to South African Minister of International Relations Ronald Lamola, and his UK counterpart, Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, the Rt Hon David Lammy to find an urgent solution to Potgieter and Huxham's ongoing detention in Equatorial Guinea.

Both ministers are in Cape Town on Tuesday, and Lamola will be speaking at the conference on Wednesday.

Previous articles:

- South Africans detained in EG: No release until Obiang gets his houses back
- UN Steps in to free two men from EG
- Families launch online campaign to free SA engineers from Equatorial Guinea
- Engineers detained in Equatorial Guinea: Government urged to help
- SA Engineers in Equatorial Guinea to appeal their shocking sentence
- SA-ingenieurs kry 12 jaar tronkstraf en moet R141-miljoen opdok
- Georgian and man from Langebaan detained in Black Beach Prison

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
